description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Arizona is a landlocked state in the Southwestern United States, sharing the Four Corners region with Colorado, New Mexico, and Utah.

description: city in and county seat of Mecklenburg County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Charlotte is the most populous city in the U.S. state of North Carolina.

description: county seat of Greenville County, South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Greenville ( GREEN-vil; locally  GREEN-vəl) is a city in Greenville County, South Carolina, United States, and its county seat.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: city and county seat of Spartanburg County, South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Spartanburg is a city in and the county seat of Spartanburg County, South Carolina, United States.

description: wife of Odysseus in Greek mythology
description_wiki: Penelope ( pə-NEL-ə-pee; Ancient Greek: Πηνελόπεια, Pēnelópeia, or Πηνελόπη, Pēnelópē) is a character in Homer's Odyssey.

Gus is a masculine name, often a diminutive for Angus, August, Augustine, Gustave, Constantine, Konstantinos, Augusten, Gustavo, Gusten, Augustus, Aengus, Argus, Fergus, Gustav, Gustafson, Ferguson, and Gussie.
Cornelius "Corneil" Vanderbilt II (November 27, 1843 – September 12, 1899) was an American business man, chairman and president of railroad lines.
Cornelius Vanderbilt (May 27, 1794 – January 4, 1877), nicknamed "the Commodore", was an American business magnate who built his wealth in railroads and shipping.

description: World War I-era training facility for the United States Army. Spartanburg, South Carolina
description_wiki: Camp Wadsworth  was a World War I-era training facility for the United States Army.
The 106th Infantry Regiment was the designation of two separate New York Army National Guard regiments.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: commemoration on 11 November of the World War I armistice
description_wiki: Armistice Day, later known as Remembrance Day in the Commonwealth and Veterans Day in the United States, is commemorated every year on 11 November to mark the armistice signed between the Allies of World War I and Germany at Compiègne, France, at 5:45 am for the cessation of hostilities on the Western Front of World War I, which took effect at 11:00 am—the "eleventh hour of the eleventh day of the eleventh month" of 1918—although, according to Thomas R. Gowenlock, an intelligence officer with the U.S. First Division, shelling from both sides continued for the rest of the day, ending only at nightfall.

surface_forms: the last day of October of that year
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: public entity for the trading of company stocks and shares
description_wiki: A stock market, equity market, or share market is the aggregation of buyers and sellers of stocks (also called as shares), which represent ownership claims on businesses; these may include securities listed on a public stock exchange as well as stock that is only traded privately, such as shares of private companies that are sold to investors through equity crowdfunding platforms.
